title: app crashes when I save
body: The app keeps crashing whenever I try to save. This is really frustrating because I keep losing my work. Please fix this ASAP.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for reporting this — sorry you're losing work, that's really frustrating.

To help us track this down, could you tell us what you're saving when the crash happens?

- A) Creating a new task
- B) Editing an existing task
- C) Updating project or account settings
- D) Something else (please describe)

Also, does it happen every single time you save, or only sometimes?

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: It happens every time I hit Save. I'm editing a task list, not settings or anything. Look, I've been using this app for months and it was fine until recently. Now it just crashes every single time. Can you just fix it?

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
That's really helpful, thank you. Since it was working fine until recently, do you know what changed around the time it started crashing?

- A) The app updated to a new version
- B) You started working with a larger task list or added something unusual (special characters, attachments, etc.)
- C) You changed browsers, devices, or operating system
- D) Nothing that you noticed — it just started happening one day

This will help us narrow down the cause quickly.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: I don't know, maybe B? I imported some data a while back, a CSV file. But it was working fine after that too... or maybe it wasn't, I don't remember exactly. I just need to save my work and the app keeps dying on me.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body:
Thanks for sticking with us on this. Based on what you've described, here's my best guess: the CSV import may have introduced some data that's causing the crash when the app tries to save.

Could you test one thing for me? Try creating a brand-new task list (just a quick test one) and saving it.

- A) The new list saves fine — only the list with imported data crashes
- B) The new list also crashes

This will tell us whether we're looking at a data problem or a code problem, and get us to a fix faster.

comments.author: reporter
comments.body: Yeah fine, a new list saves fine. It's only the big list that crashes. So what, I have to just delete all my work? That's not a solution.

comments.author: triage-agent
comments.body: [RESOLVED] {"title":"Crash on save when editing task list containing CSV-imported data","problem":"The application crashes every time the user attempts to save edits to a task list that contains data imported from a CSV file. Newly created task lists save without issue. The user reports this started recently, though the CSV import happened some time ago and may not have been the immediate trigger.","root_cause_hypothesis":"The CSV import likely introduced data that the save/serialization path cannot handle — possible causes include special characters, excessively long fields, malformed or unexpected data types, or a record count that exceeds a size/memory limit in the save operation. A recent app update may have introduced a regression in how such edge-case data is processed during save.","reproduction_steps":["Identify or obtain a task list that contains CSV-imported data (or import a CSV file into a new task list)","Edit any task in that list","Click Save","Observe crash — the app terminates and unsaved work is lost"],"environment":"Not specified — but the issue is data-dependent rather than environment-dependent, as confirmed by the isolation test (new lists save fine on the same setup)","severity":"high","impact":"User is completely unable to save edits to their primary task list, causing repeated data loss. This is a blocking issue for their daily workflow.","recommended_fix":"Investigate the save/serialization code path for task lists. Compare the data in the crashing list against a working list to identify problematic records or fields. Add defensive handling (input sanitization, size limits, error boundaries) so malformed data causes a graceful error rather than a crash. Check recent commits to the save path for regressions in data handling.","proposed_test_case":"Import a CSV file containing edge-case data (special characters, very long strings, empty fields, non-UTF-8 characters, large row counts) into a task list, then verify that saving edits succeeds or fails gracefully with a user-facing error message rather than crashing.","information_gaps":["Exact error message or stack trace from the crash","Contents/structure of the CSV file that was imported","Browser/platform details (though likely irrelevant given this is data-dependent)","Exact app version and whether a recent update correlates with the onset"]}
